title: Add tier classification at task creation
context: Tasks need model_tier set before the first workflow step runs. Currently model_tier is always null at creation. The field exists on TaskCreate schema (schemas.py line 64) and Task model. Design doc: docs/task-tier-classification-and-session-cleanup-design.md. Use existing llm_direct module (app/orchestrator/llm_direct.py) for local qwen call — it already handles LM Studio with timeout+fallback.
spec: 1. Create app/services/tier_classifier.py with async function classify_task_tier(task_dict: dict, db: AsyncSession) -> str. Logic: (a) if task_dict.get('model_tier') → return it unchanged. (b) Project hard minimum: if project_id contains 'lobs-server', 'lobs-mission-control', or 'lobs-mobile' → floor is 'standard'. (c) Call llm_direct with system prompt defining tiers (small=boilerplate/docs/simple crud/scripts/experiments, standard=production features/API/refactors/research, strong=architecture/security/complex debugging/migrations) and user prompt with title+notes+project_id+agent. Parse first word of response, validate is one of small/standard/strong. On any exception or invalid response → use 'standard'. (d) Apply project floor: if floor='standard' and result='small' → return 'standard'. 2. In app/routers/tasks.py create_task(), after db.flush()+db.refresh(db_task), call classify_task_tier and set db_task.model_tier. Wrap in try/except — on any error log warning and continue (do not fail the request). 3. The TaskCreate schema already has model_tier: Optional[str] = None — callers can pass it to override.
acceptance: POST /api/tasks with no model_tier → response has model_tier populated. POST with model_tier='strong' → keeps 'strong'. lobs-server project gets minimum 'standard'. LLM unavailable → gets 'standard', no error. Unit tests in tests/test_tier_classifier.py covering all 4 paths.

title: Fix NodeHandlers.delete_session() to actually delete sessions
context: Session cleanup nodes call NodeHandlers.delete_session() which is a no-op placeholder (workflow_nodes.py lines 201-211). The real delete implementation exists in WorkerGateway.delete_session() (worker_gateway.py lines 382-422) using WebSocket JSON-RPC sessions.delete — this method is already proven via worker_monitor.py line 366. The task-router done node already has correct session_refs wired for all spawn nodes. Only the underlying delete call is broken. Design doc: docs/task-tier-classification-and-session-cleanup-design.md
spec: In app/orchestrator/workflow_nodes.py, replace NodeHandlers.delete_session() no-op (lines ~201-211) with: check self.worker_manager has gateway attribute → call await self.worker_manager.gateway.delete_session(session_key) → log result. If no gateway available, log warning and return (do not raise). Also remove dead code: @register_node('classify_model_tier') and _node_classify_model_tier function (~line 1643) — no workflow uses this node type.
acceptance: After a programmer task completes and hits the done/cleanup node, the worker session is deleted from Gateway (verify via openclaw sessions list — session count stays low). No crash when gateway unavailable. Unit test: mock worker_manager.gateway, assert delete_session called with correct session key.
